Admission Related.question: What is the detailed admission procedure for undergraduate courses?
Admission Related.answer: To get admission in undergraduate courses, candidates must apply through the central university portal. Cutoff marks are announced in 3-4 successive rounds based on merit. Once selected, applicants must visit the Admission Block for document verification and pay the tuition fees.
Admission Related.location: Admission Cell, Ground Floor, Admin Block (Block A)
Admission Related.procedure:
1. Fill out the application form on the university website.
2. Wait for the cutoff announcement and check selection status.
3. Report to the Admission Cell Room 101 with all original and copy documents.
4. Complete physical verification.
5. Pay the fee via the online portal to secure your seat.
Admission Related.deadlines: Typically July 31st of each academic year
Admission Related.additional_instructions: Bring at least two sets of self-attested photocopies of all listed documents.

Admission Related.question: What are the eligibility criteria and documents required for PG admissions?
Admission Related.answer: Postgraduate admission requires a completed Bachelor's degree in the relevant discipline with a minimum of 55% aggregate marks (50% for reserved categories) and a qualifying score in the University Entrance Exam (CS-PET).
Admission Related.location: Postgraduate Admissions Wing, Room 105, Admin Block
Admission Related.procedure:
1. Register and clear the entrance exam.
2. Register for counselling on the PG portal.
3. Present degree credentials at PG Wing.
4. Receive allotment letter and pay fee.
Admission Related.approval_authority: Head of Department (Concerned) & PG Admission Board
Admission Related.additional_instructions: Make sure your degree certificate is issued by a UGC recognized university.

Marks Related.question: How do I check my semester CGPA and grade cards?
Marks Related.answer: Semester grade sheets are published on the Student Portal (LMS) under the 'Academics' tab. You can download unofficial copies instantly or request official transcript prints.
Marks Related.location: Controller of Examinations (CoE) Office, Block B
Marks Related.procedure:
1. Login to the LMS portal.
2. Click on 'Academics' and select 'Grade Report'.
3. Click on the respective semester to view CGPA.
4. For official signed grade sheets, fill the request form at the CoE window.
Marks Related.additional_instructions: Keep your password secure. Do not share portal login credentials.

Marks Related.question: What is the procedure to apply for answer sheet re-evaluation?
Marks Related.answer: Students unsatisfied with their grades can apply for re-evaluation or photocopy of the answer script. A non-refundable fee of $20 per subject applies.
Marks Related.procedure:
1. Download the Re-evaluation form from the student resources page.
2. Pay the re-evaluation fee online via portal.
3. Submit the filled form with receipt to the Evaluation Cell.
4. Wait for the re-evaluated grade to reflect on the portal.
Marks Related.additional_instructions: Note that marks may increase, decrease, or remain unchanged after re-evaluation.

Address Related.question: How can I change my permanent or communication address in college records?
Address Related.answer: Students can update their address in the college ERP. A change of permanent address requires official residential proof.
required_documents: Valid Proof (Aadhaar Card, Passport, or Electricity Bill)
Address Related.location: Student Records Section, Room 102, Admin Block
Address Related.procedure:
1. Draft a request letter and attach residential proofs.
2. Submit documents to the Records Desk.
3. The staff will verify documents and update the database.
Address Related.additional_instructions: Make sure the name on the address proof matches your admission name exactly.

Library Related.question: How to get a library membership card and what are the borrowing rules?
Library Related.answer: All enrolled students are eligible for free library membership. Students receive a library barcode on their Student ID card, allowing them to borrow up to 4 books simultaneously.
Library Related.location: Library Circulation Counter, Central Library Block
Library Related.procedure:
1. Visit the library membership counter.
2. Submit the registration form and ID card.
3. The librarian will activate your barcode and issue the borrower logbook.
Library Related.deadlines: Within 1 month of joining the college
Library Related.additional_instructions: Books can be borrowed for 14 days. A late fee of $0.50 per day applies per book afterwards.

Scholarship Related.question: How to apply for the Merit-cum-Means College Scholarship?
Scholarship Related.answer: The Merit-cum-Means scholarship provides up to 50% tuition waiver to deserving students with excellent academic records and family income below $8,000 per annum.
Scholarship Related.procedure:
1. Collect the application form from Room 102 or download it from the college portal.
2. Get the Bonafide Certificate signed from Academic Cell.
3. Compile all documents and submit the physical folder at Scholarship Desk.
4. Verification will be performed, followed by interview rounds.
Scholarship Related.additional_instructions: Ensure your bank account is active and linked with your Aadhaar number.

Scholarship Related.question: What is the procedure for National Scholarship Portal (NSP) verification?
Scholarship Related.answer: NSP applications must be filled online and then submitted physically to the institute nodal officer for digital verification.
Scholarship Related.location: Academic Desk 2, ground floor, Block A
Scholarship Related.procedure:
1. Apply online on the NSP portal and choose our institute.
2. Print the submitted application.
3. Submit the printout and copies of documents at Desk 2.
4. Nodal officer will verify your records online.
Scholarship Related.deadlines: Subject to central government guidelines (typically October 31st)
Scholarship Related.additional_instructions: Check your application status regularly on the NSP login page.

Abroad Studies.question: What are the student exchange programs and how to apply?
Abroad Studies.answer: We have partnerships with universities in Europe and the USA for semester-long exchange programs. Students in their 5th or 6th semester are eligible.
Abroad Studies.location: International Relations Office (IRO), Room 302, Block C
Abroad Studies.procedure:
1. Read the list of partner universities on the IRO board.
2. Submit the interest application along with your SOP and HoD NOC.
3. Attend the selection panel interview.
4. If selected, apply directly to the host university under our nomination.
Abroad Studies.deadlines: January 15th for Fall Semester, August 15th for Spring Semester
Abroad Studies.additional_instructions: Host university tuition fee is waived, but living expenses must be borne by the student unless external funding is secured.

Hostel Related.question: What is the leave application process for hostellers?
Hostel Related.answer: Hostel residents must submit a formal leave request before leaving the campus overnight. Outings during weekends require parental notification.
Hostel Related.location: Chief Warden's Office, Hostel Block C (Ground Floor)
Hostel Related.procedure:
1. Fill in the leave request details on the Hostel Portal.
2. Ensure your parents send a confirmation email/message to the warden.
3. Visit the Warden Office to get the physical Outing Pass signed.
4. Present the pass at the campus main security gate when leaving.
Hostel Related.deadlines: At least 24 hours prior to departure
Hostel Related.additional_instructions: The maximum permissible duration for local leaves is 5 consecutive days. In case of medical emergencies, notify immediately.

Hostel Related.question: What are the rules regarding parents staying in the hostel?
Hostel Related.answer: Parents are not allowed inside the student hostel rooms. However, they can book a room in the university guest house or meet students in the visitor lounge.
Hostel Related.procedure:
1. Register parents at the hostel gate security logbook.
2. Accompany them to the visitor lounge.
3. For overnight stay, book a guest house room via the estate office in advance.
Hostel Related.deadlines: Guest house booking: 3 days in advance
Hostel Related.additional_instructions: Male parents are strictly prohibited in the girls' hostels and vice-versa.

Fees Section.question: How to pay college semester fees and what are the payment modes?
Fees Section.answer: Semester fees must be paid online via the student ERP portal. Cash payments are not accepted, but DD (Demand Drafts) are accepted in exceptional cases.
Fees Section.procedure:
1. Log into the Student portal and click 'Fees'.
2. View current invoice and select 'Pay Online'.
3. Choose Netbanking, UPI, or Credit/Debit card.
4. Download the generated receipt instantly.
Fees Section.additional_instructions: Late fee of $5 per day applies after the deadline. Always save and print the online transaction receipt.

Certificates.question: What is the process to apply for a Bonafide Certificate?
Certificates.answer: A Bonafide Certificate is issued for academic, visa, or loan purposes, confirming the student's enrollment status.
Certificates.procedure:
1. Collect the application form from Desk 1 or download it from portal.
2. Fill the form, stating the purpose (e.g. passport application, education loan).
3. Attach fee receipt copy and ID card.
4. Submit at Desk 1. Collect after registration and signatures.
Certificates.additional_instructions: If applying for a loan, ensure bank details/requirements are correctly specified on the application form.

Certificates.question: What is the process to replace a lost College ID Card?
Certificates.answer: Students who lose their ID cards must report it to security, file a diary, pay a reissue fee of $10, and submit a replacement form.
Certificates.location: Security and Estates Office, Ground floor, Block A
Certificates.procedure:
1. Register a lost report at the campus main security desk.
2. Pay the replacement fee at the accounts window.
3. Fill in the replacement card form.
4. Submit everything to the Security Office and collect the new card once printed.
Certificates.deadlines: Within 7 days of losing the card
Certificates.additional_instructions: Temporary library gates access slip can be obtained from the chief librarian in the interim.

Academic Problems.question: What is the procedure to apply for a subject change or elective swap?
Academic Problems.answer: Elective subject swaps are permitted only in the first week of the semester, subject to seat availability.
Academic Problems.procedure:
1. Fill the Course Swap Form.
2. Take consent/signatures from both course teachers and department heads.
3. Submit the signed form to the Academic Registrar.
4. Check your portal database for course update within 2 days.
Academic Problems.deadlines: Strictly within 7 calendar days of semester start
Academic Problems.additional_instructions: Swaps are not permitted for core mandatory subjects.

Staff Office Addresses.question: Where are the key administrative and academic offices located?
Staff Office Addresses.answer: Administrative offices (Registrar, Accounts, HR) are in the Admin Block (Block A). Dean and Department Head offices are located on the second floor of their respective academic blocks.
Staff Office Addresses.location: Admin Block (Block A) and Academic Blocks C, D, E
Staff Office Addresses.procedure:
1. Consult the digital campus map at the entrance.
2. Visit Block A for core administration and finance queries.
3. Visit Block C for Engineering, Block D for Humanities, and Block E for Sciences.
Staff Office Addresses.additional_instructions: Appointments are highly recommended for meeting heads of departments.

Food Court Details.question: What are the food courts, faculty dining locations, and hours?
Food Court Details.answer: The university has 3 food courts. A dedicated Faculty and Staff Dining Area is located in the Central Food Court on the second floor.
Food Court Details.procedure:
1. Present your faculty card at the cash register to obtain meal coupons.
2. Access the dedicated faculty lounge area for dining services.
Food Court Details.additional_instructions: Special catering requests for events must be filed 3 days in advance.

Library Timings.question: What are the faculty library timings and journal access details?
Library Timings.answer: Faculty members have 24/7 access to the Digital Library portal and extended borrowing privileges of up to 15 books for 90 days.
Library Timings.timings: Physical Wing: 08:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Digital Portal: 24/7
Library Timings.procedure:
1. Request library registration at checkout counter.
2. Collect digital credentials for IEEE, Elsevier, and Springer journal access.
3. Check out books using the dedicated faculty kiosk.
Library Timings.additional_instructions: Renewals can be done online through the library catalogue interface.

Academic Section.question: What is the procedure for updating student marks and attendance on the ERP?
Academic Section.answer: Attendance and internal marks must be entered regularly on the ERP portal. Attendance must be locked weekly.
Academic Section.location: ERP Support Center, Room 204, Block A
Academic Section.procedure:
1. Log into ERP with institutional credentials.
2. Select 'My Classes' and select the course code.
3. Click 'Attendance' or 'Grade Entry'.
4. Save, review, and click 'Publish' (Note: published grades cannot be changed without written approval).
Academic Section.approval_authority: Controller of Examinations & Head of Department
Academic Section.deadlines: Attendance: Every Sunday by 06:00 PM. Exam grades: Within 5 days of exam completion
Academic Section.additional_instructions: Ensure you maintain a physical register of marks and attendance for final audits.

Department Information.question: How to apply for internal research grants and lab funding?
Department Information.answer: Faculty members are encouraged to apply for the annual University Research Initiative (URI) grants, providing funding up to $25,000.
Department Information.location: Research & Development Cell, Room 304, Admin Block
Department Information.procedure:
1. Download the URI Proposal template.
2. Submit proposal draft to Department Research Committee (DRC) for peer review.
3. Incorporate feedback and submit final proposal to R&D Cell.
4. Defend proposal before the expert committee.
Department Information.processing_time: 45 days for review and funding release
Department Information.additional_instructions: Projects involving external collaborators must attach formal letters of support.

Guest Room Details.question: How can parents book guest rooms on campus?
Guest Room Details.answer: The university provides fully furnished guest rooms for parents visiting their children. Booking must be initiated online or via written request.
Guest Room Details.location: Estate and Logistics Desk, Block A
Guest Room Details.procedure:
1. Fill the Guest Room Booking Form on the Parent Portal.
2. Upload identity documents.
3. Pay the room charge of $15 per day online.
4. Check-in at the guest house desk with physical Aadhaar copy.
Guest Room Details.deadlines: Book at least 3 days prior to arrival
Guest Room Details.additional_instructions: Maximum stay duration is 3 consecutive nights. Meals are charged separately at the dining hall.

Admission Office.question: Where is the parent helpline and admission query desk located?
Admission Office.answer: A dedicated Parent Desk is located at the main entry of the Admission Block to resolve queries regarding fees, registrations, and courses.
Admission Office.location: Desk A-1, Reception Lobby, Admission Block
Admission Office.procedure:
1. Visit the reception lobby.
2. State query to the helpline desk agent.
3. Receive printed procedure slips or meet admission officers.

Food Court.question: Can parents/visitors dine at campus food courts?
Food Court.answer: Yes, all campus dining areas and food courts are open to parents and visitors. Payments can be made via cash, cards, or digital UPI apps.
Food Court.location: All Food Courts (Central, Hostels, and North Block)
Food Court.procedure:
1. Visit any food court counter.
2. Place orders directly.
3. Pay via standard payment modes.
Food Court.additional_instructions: Dining inside individual student hostel messes requires purchasing a visitor coupon at the hostel entrance.

Hostel Details.question: What are hostel visiting hours and warden contact details?
Hostel Details.answer: Parents can meet their children in the designated hostel reception lobby during specified hours.
Hostel Details.timings: Visiting Hours: 04:00 PM – 07:00 PM (Weekdays), 10:00 AM – 07:00 PM (Sundays)
Hostel Details.procedure:
1. Present your ID card to the security warden at the gate.
2. Obtain a visitor pass.
3. Student will be summoned to the lobby.
4. Return pass when exiting.
Hostel Details.additional_instructions: For emergency contact, wardens' office numbers are displayed on the parent portal under 'Hostel Info'.

Student Marks Guidance.question: How can parents track their child's academic progress?
Student Marks Guidance.answer: Parents are provided with a dedicated Parent Portal Login, sending monthly reports on attendance and marks directly to registered mobile/emails.
Student Marks Guidance.procedure:
1. Open the parent portal login page.
2. Enter registered mobile/email and verify with OTP.
3. Click 'Attendance & Grades' to view detailed trackers.
Student Marks Guidance.additional_instructions: If you notice less than 75% attendance alert, please contact the class mentor immediately.

Parent Meeting Details.question: When are Parent-Teacher Meetings (PTMs) scheduled and how to attend?
Parent Meeting Details.answer: Official Parent-Teacher Meetings are scheduled once per semester, typically after mid-term examination results. Parents can join physically or virtually.
Parent Meeting Details.procedure:
1. Receive meeting notification from your child's department.
2. RVSP with your preferred mode (physical or Zoom online link).
3. Meet department faculty to discuss academic and overall progress.
Parent Meeting Details.deadlines: Register/RSVP within 3 days of invitation receipt
Parent Meeting Details.additional_instructions: If you cannot attend, you can schedule a private virtual call with the student mentor by emailing them.
